transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Oh, you're muted.
I said, how are you?
What's going on?
Good.
Chugging along.
I feel like I blinked and it's almost February.
Insane, isn't it?
It's crazy.
How are you doing?
You holding up all right?
Yeah, everything's good.
Sweet.
Is it just us today?
Um, no, Ali said he's running...
I'm little over, but he will join.
Going and started.
No stress.
All right.
Happy to jump in.
I know I've sent a ton of different updates to you guys in Slack over this last week, trying to make a lot of progress on some strategy pieces and performance reporting.
And highlight for you the performance report that I shared earlier.
It's got a bunch of different graphs and talking points that can be pulled for LinkedIn.
I made sure to work with my team.
So anything in there is fair game for you to use for any posts that you want to keep going with.
Yeah.
No, that took us forever to get to you.
Appreciate your patience.
But there's a lot of good stuff for you to pull in there.
So hopefully that's helpful.
Sounds good.
Cool, cool.
So the other thing I wanted to make sure that we talk through today is that Reddit strategy that I sent over.
I know we've been talking a little bit about, you know, how to sort of.
Push the envelope with LLM performance and that you guys are wanting to get onto Reddit.

So there's a couple of action items in that realm that I wanted to talk about and just kind of clarify a timeline on what it's going to look like for us to get a pipeline up and running and actually producing content for that.
Otherwise, this is just kind of a quick recap of a lot of things.
Any questions about anything that we've sent over, things that have happened in the last couple weeks before I jump into some of the nitty gritty discussion?
I don't think so.
Cool.
Okay.
Have you guys had a chance to look over the performance report that I sent over?
Sure.
Okay.
No worries.
know things are I don't even see it.
Where is it?
Is it on our shared channel?
It is.
Yeah.
I sent it in a message just a couple of days ago, but I will re-tag it when I'll do a call recap and I'll tag it in there for you as well.
So you've got it.
Okay.
Perfect.
All We'll see be back.
Thank Sure.
But it's fairly extensive.
So some highlights before I dive too, too far into it.
A lot of accounts saw kind of a dip over the holidays.
But one thing that we really liked seeing is that post-holiday traffic, you actually hit an all-time high in sessions, which is really great.
So it shows that, like, we didn't actually lose momentum.
That's functionality, a little blip on the radar.
And we're continuing to see, like, really strong growth.
And one big highlight is, like, keyword ranking.
So any keywords where you have content showing up within the first page of Google, those have skyrocketed since we started publishing.
I mean, keeping in mind that we started from essentially zero with organic keywords.
But seeing some really good progress there for what we'd want to see around a three-month mark.

Basically showing that, like, everything that we're working on in terms of building authority and some of the topic areas that we're going after, Google is starting to recognize that there's a lot of authority that you're...
Your site offers and that it's worth putting it on the first page of Google results for folks to start taking a look at.

And speaking of LLM traffic, one thing that we saw as far as referrals, so anytime that someone gets to an understory blog post from an LLM, we saw some really good growth there over the last three months as well.
And there's actually higher engagement across content when they find it from, say, like, ChatGPT or Claude than when they find it from Google, which is really nice.
So it's telling us that...
Like in terms of dwell time and things like that?
Yeah, exactly.
So when people find a blog post from you guys on a large language model, they're sticking around a little bit longer, they're engaging with it a little bit further.
So it tells us that the LLMs are recognizing, like, the relevant audience to put it in front of, which is nice to see, too.
Like I mentioned, there's lots of stuff in here.
I would definitely encourage...
If you have any questions about any of these stats, as you're looking through it, can just comment directly in here.
I'm happy to follow up async, or we can talk about it a little bit more in our next call as well.
But basically, the short note is that for the last three months, we're seeing all the right signals that we want to.
Everything is growing, like impressions, clicks, sessions.

We do want to start optimizing for clicks to drive that a little bit more, as well as continue to optimize content for large language models, which is one of my discussion points I wanted to chat about today, as far as how we're looking at refreshing content and some specific things we can do, building into putting TLDRs and FAQs in content that LLMs really like to see, now that we've got some authority and some traction, starting to just continue to compound that momentum.
So, a couple different things.
This is showing, basically, the total.
Sessions of folks looking at your content, and then engagement is looking at how long they're spending on a page reading it.
And we're seeing an increase in both of those.
This here is that little seasonality dip, which we saw across industries.
This is actually not the most dramatic dip that I've seen.
And like I flagged earlier for you, Alex, seeing this rise back up above performance in early December is a really good sign that we haven't actually lost momentum.
Folks just get distracted over Christmas.
And then a couple other things I wanted to call out.
We also look here at specific groups of content that are performing really well.
So some things that I thought was really interesting is that as far as trends and benchmark content, that's been really popular and getting a lot of attention.
So with future assignments and keyword research, we want to lean in here, as well as the B2B SaaS growth strategy content that we've developed.
That's also been performing really well.
People love benchmark reports.
So some opportunities for us there.
Yeah, I think it's one of our very top, yeah, this like benchmark conversion rate.
They're all for it.
So it takes a little bit of finessing because we don't want to like link out to a bunch of other industry reports.
So we have to be a little bit careful about where we pull that data from so that when we do link out to external sites, we're not sending them to a competitor.
But yeah, exciting to see that those are getting so much traction.
Especially with the new year, imagine there's a lot of folks wanting to know how to, you know, rank their performance and all those different pieces.
So another piece I was highlighting earlier as far as like keyword positions.
So this is telling us whenever an article shows up, if someone plugs a search into Google, if it's anywhere in position one through 10, this is meaning it's on the first page of Google results.
And so that is captured.
Rank 1, really hard to achieve.
It's great to see this growth initially.

And then everything in purple, we're seeing a huge spike in keywords that are tracking anywhere from 2 to 10 on that first page of Google, which is really great to see at this point in our content as well.
And so early on, were having manually index a lot of articles to make sure that Google recognized it.
And now it's actually putting it in front of people's eyeballs.
Do you know the split between brand and non-brand search term?
So for this one, I believe that this is looking at GrowthX specific.
I will, I'll go back and confirm.
We spliced it a couple different ways on our Looker dashboard, which I think you guys have access to.
And so if you want to dig into that, you can always, I'll show you really quick.
Let's see here.
So if I go in here, so anything on this?
Keywords, this will be branded content included in like query ranking distribution.
But if you want to see just like growthx specific content, you can always come up here and get rid of that and then adjust these dates here.
And for session medium, like if you want to filter out like just see organic traffic, sometimes we'll have like paid media and things in here as well, depending on how GA4 is set up.
And then if you want to look at non-branded specifically, it's going to be the second tab.
And again, you can filter out non-growthx specific.
When it comes to filtering out like here's because of the filters that we have to pull out non-branded keywords.
But then there's an overview like landing pages are ranking.
And here's that chart that I showed you as far as where it's grouping in those different positions.
Is that helpful?
Yep.
Yeah.
I was thinking, just going back to the first report, I was thinking you were filtering by keywords, but you're saying you're filtering by lane.
Like the content.
Yeah.
So yeah, that makes sense.
Exactly.
Yeah.
And like I said, there's, you know, there's views if you want to look at like keywords in general on the overall or, yeah, those non-branded here.
couple of different ways we can splice them.
Yeah.
And so this is, well, so what this is showing, there might be multiple articles that, or an article that ranks for multiple different keywords, just because of how content is written.
So this is keywords in general, not necessarily like total article count.
I want to make sure that's clear.
So one article might show up for like three keywords and it might rank differently depending on what someone searches.
So if they search like best B2B SaaS marketing agency, we might have an article that ranks on page five, but if they search maybe a more niche term, it might show up on the first page.
Does that make
Makes sense?
Yeah.
Yeah, it does.
And then one other highlight.
We're seeing some good results in referral traffic from LLMs as well.
So, I mean, October, obviously, this was our baseline where we started.
We've gone all the to like 65 sessions.
And I think I flagged, I'm not sure if you were on right when we were chatting about this, but the traffic that these LLMs are driving is more engaged.
I think that was right when you jumped in and came on.
So seeing a solid engagement rate is really nice to see across LLM traffic as well.
And then there's some, we pull out like specific pages here that are ranking well.
So any blogs, and then there's a few that it's not clear where on the site it came from because the LLMs don't always track specifically.
But we've got a couple of blogs that are driving some solid LLM traffic.
Again, those benchmark pieces, people really liked that as well there.
Questions about any of that?
I don't have any questions about that.
mean, let's say I want to know how we double it.
But I do have specific other questions related to other stuff, too.
But no, I think it all sounds good.
I don't know.
Yeah, and obviously, like, we, you know, part of the intention of our content engine is to create compounding growth so that, you know, between now and six months, it should continue to rise.
And one of the advantages of organic traffic is you keep that retention once you've developed that content, right?
Whereas, like, paid media, you dump a bunch of money into a few specific keywords, and you get that hockey stick effect where it spikes really rapidly.
But you have to keep paying in order to keep that attention in those eyeballs.
Yeah, for sure.
So.
So.
So.
So.
You mentioned you've got some specific questions.
I had a few things as far as strategy that I'm looking at going forward, but if you want to go through some of your questions first, I'm happy to do that.
Yeah, they should be pretty quick.
So one thing I was noticing is that it looks like we have a bunch of pages that aren't being indexed.
I don't know if you're aware of that, but I was just looking in Search Console.
There's like 82 pages that aren't being indexed.
I'm not exactly sure why.
A lot of them have like 404s and stuff like that, but yeah, I'm not sure if you have any insights there.
Yeah, I can take a closer look.
I know for our blog content specifically, we do some manually requesting of indexing when we go to publish.
Let me see.
There's a whole variety of reasons that pages might not index.
That's
I can have my team, my team and I will look into this a little bit more to see if there's patterns that we're noticing.
I know we had to do some fixes early on as far as like your sitemap to make sure that things were mapped correctly because that can have an impact.
And I will say there's, I've seen on a lot of different accounts where, you know, about half of their pages aren't indexed for a variety of different reasons.
Sometimes it's because they're not optimized to be indexed just in the way the page is set up.
Or it's not like a primary page.
But I can get you some more information on that for sure.
that's good.
And then the other thing was about Reddit.
So I'm looking at your message.
So we should be all good for Reddit.
Yeah, there's a couple action items that I had down here further.
As far as Reddit goes, there's a few next steps that I flagged in here and I can highlight these in a...
Chat as well.
So on your page, I think you still need to accept the change to the email.
You should have gotten a request for that.
If I look in settings, I think you just need to confirm.
I can resend this right now if you want me to.
Just so that the ownership is moved over to you.
And then I believe I sent you the password as well in a DM.
So if you need that again, let me know.
We'll use that password to log in.
So if you ever change it, just keep me posted.
Yeah, we'll see what happens because I already had an account linked to that email.
Okay.
If it fusses at you, let me know.
And I can change it to pretty much anything you guys want.
I had just squatted it with our GrowthX email so that I could move quickly because it needed an initial confirmation.
As far as optimizations, before you guys start running ads, we do...
Do you need a banner image?
And I have the specific size that that would be good just as far as like something for your logo.
I tried to snag one from your website, but it was just not quite the right dimensions.
And so it was cutting off the brand in a funky way.
Yeah.
Can you send it to Alex?
Because I don't think it's working.
Yeah.
Maybe, Alex, do you need to like use your email for Reddit?
Well, I guess we'll just like share the password.
Yeah, exactly.
Let me, I'll faff with this.
I can get this over to you right after the call.
It might also show up in like spam or updates.
Sometimes it hides from me.
And then the other thing that I was looking at, making sure it's set up for social links.
I did set up your YouTube and your podcasts.
There's not an option on here for LinkedIn, but if you guys wanted to link to a LinkedIn, just depends on how you want to frame that in here.
There you you go.
There There you go.
go.

And then, yeah, so something to keep in mind, one thing that we'll be working on as we build up these posts is working to build up this karma before we would want to start posting, like, you know, avoiding spamming any kind of subreddits because you can really easily get banned if Reddit thinks that you're a bot.
And so in posting here, that'll help avoid any kind of ban risk.
So something to look into with your paid strategy as well to make sure that it's not potentially going to flag.
And if you want an example of kind of the long run of what we're looking at building, SEMrush has, this is Mute Dash, they've got a really good account where, where, I just had it pulled up.
I think I have it linked.

So this is a good example of kind of where we're looking at building towards two in the long run, where this is their user profile, and then you can see that they've got a lot of different posts on a variety of topics, optimized, lots of good karma built up to help avoid any kind of ban risks.
And one of the advantages of Reddit from like an SEOs love to cite Reddit as a source.
So if we can put things on here that folks want to read, that LLMs want to pick up, that's a potential avenue for more traffic as well.
Any other Reddit questions?
So just so I'm clear, will you be posting on behalf of us on Reddit like the same way?
Yeah, so the way the strategy lines it out is essentially like in this post section, we need to build out a path.
And start testing some calibration articles and research specific topics, because they're going to be slightly different than what we'd want to put on your website.
And then, yeah, like once a week, once we scale, we would put a new post up here.
So on your account, it would, if you click on view profile, it would live under here.
Got it.
Awesome.
Super exciting.
Yeah.
Excited to get it going.
And then we'll figure out the best way to report on it.
But Reddit reporting is a little bit funky and different than how we've got set up for other SEO content.
So we're still looking at the best way to do that.
But timeline, expect like mid to late February for these posts to start going up.
Just up to run smoothly.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Cool.
The other thing, a couple other updates.
We are currently finishing up testing some automation for publishing.
No action needed from you guys specifically.
You might see some test posts go up that we'll take down right away just as we're finalizing that step.
Basically just helps us be more efficient as we produce content and get it pushed out for y'all.

And then one of the things that I'm looking at going into February as well is doing a deeper dive into content that we've already published, reviewing, you know, we've got lots of articles that have lots of good traffic.
And so now at this point that we've got a good density of content, we can start to look at what content isn't performing the way we'd like it to, to potentially do some optimization.
And prune a little bit as well.
Things like, you know, not just updating images, but like I mentioned, we can start looking at adding in TLDRs and FAQs and some of those different aspects as well.
Or potentially the direction we took the content initially wasn't quite what people wanted to read.
And so that needs some refinement.
So we'll be looking at that through February and probably starting to do some more refreshes in March.
Okay, sounds good.
And then I don't know if you guys.
Saw, we did a bunch of keyword research as well for the next month to plan out assignments.
A lot of what we looked at were those key areas where we're seeing really good performance in terms of benchmark content as well as growth strategy pieces.
So I don't know if you guys wanted to look over those keywords.
We're happy to just keep running with them.
We basically cross-referenced competitor keywords where other folks are ranking really well that have strong volume, especially as you're starting to build some site authority.
But if you're happy, we can just run with those.
So would those replace the current ones or be in addition to?
They're in addition to.
So because our content engine has been running super smoothly, we had actually gotten through a lot of the assignments that we had already generated and done keyword research around.
So this is the next batch that we're looking at producing.
Yeah, it would be great to see that new list and then just a refresh on what we have been targeting.
Yeah, totally.
I can write a little audit for you and have that to you probably by the end of next week.

If you want to look at the initial assignments that we've got developed, I sort of have some split up here, and I can send you this link as well, but it's basically split them up into some of the different clusters that we're looking at targeting with an example, and I'll make sure that all the new content that I've planned out is in here.
There's a few that I had already pushed through for the next month, which is in this content calendar.
So both those views give a good sense of what topics we're looking at specifically.
And I can add in a little checklist if there's anything that you don't want us to focus on.
I'll just add a little flag, and if you just click on that flag, then I'll know to remove it and schedule something else in.
Yep, that sounds good.
Cool.
All right.
I know that you guys are also working on adding in some, like, LinkedIn thought leadership as a service.
I was curious if you have any messaging that
You can offer as far as how you're framing that with leads or prospects, because I'd love to start incorporating some keywords around that service as well into our content.
So we can highlight if so long as you guys are ready to start talking about it.
But having some of your messaging and positioning would be helpful so that I can start teaching our LOM or our pipelines how to talk about it.
We will have it very soon.
Cool.
Yeah.
Awesome.
Just wanted to flag.
I'll start doing some keyword research, but I'll hold off on actually putting those into our calendar for now, just so we can have that kind of in the background pipeline.
Are there any other initiatives that we should know about similar to that service you guys are working on launching?
HubSpot work, maybe.
Yeah, that's exactly what I was going to say.
Anything like ads and just anything related to ads and HubSpot or outbound and HubSpot?
Spot.
We work with a tool called Outbound Sync, like articles about stuff like that.
Cool.
I know my keyword research site actually looked at some, like incorporating some of the tools that you guys use as well.
So some of that is actually in the works and in some of the assignments that we've got generated.
So glad to hear that we're on the right track there.
But yeah, I can make sure that some of this is incorporated.
Like I'll do, like I said, in my audit and come through.
Good.
Cool.
And I guess the other thing is, I know I brought it up before.
It's not something that's in scope necessarily with you guys, but just like thinking about backlinks and like ways to approach that.
Yes.
Yeah.
So I'm actually getting some more information from my team.
One of the things that I talked about with, as we did the performance audit, our recommendation would be to try and aim for like five backlinks a month.
And I'm checking on.
And pricing for that, if that's a service that we've still got going.
I know we've done it in the past, and there's some conversation around the optimization of it.
So I'm hoping to have an answer for you on that by next week, if that works for you.
Yeah, I mean, I'm curious to know what that service would be.
Are you just reaching out on behalf of companies in order to place our content on their websites?
Or what do you guys actually do as part of that?
Yeah, so we essentially act as a middleman in that role, where we reach out.
We try and match companies based on specific industries that you're in and authority to make sure that we're backlinking where it strategically makes sense.
And then we coordinate what those backlinks look like as far as keywords that get planted and what articles are getting linked to to help build that out.
What does that usually look like?
Is that like, hey, we noticed you have a blog, we'd love to feature on that blog.
What exactly do those negotiations sound like?
Because, you we also could reach out to people on our network just to get some content live on their sites.
But I'm just curious what you usually do.
Yeah.
So we have some set up where, like, we have some informal conversations.
As far as, like, specific backlink services, I believe we have some set agreements.
There's another group that helps coordinate some of that, I believe.
And essentially, it's a system where we know specific groups that have already agreed to support backlinking.
And so we match companies within those industries.
That's my best understanding.
I haven't been as hands-on with the backlinking process on my side here.
Okay.
Yeah.
No worries.
I'm just thinking we have a pretty extensive partner network.
So, you know, Alex, I'm just thinking, like, how we can leverage all of those connections to just feature on their site and what people will usually ask in those situations.
Yeah.
I can use some messaging as far as some of the more informal asks that we've had.
If that would be helpful, I can shoot that over to you.
It's pretty straightforward.
It's like, hey.
Hey, here's the content that we've got.

One of the things we like to try and do in those situations is offer some priority content, maybe that we want to start getting recognized or content that's built around specific keywords that we want to flag for.
And see if they have relevant blog posts where they might be working on with some super relevant anchor text so that as we're linking out to that content, it aligns well, if that makes sense.
Yeah, totally.
Yeah.
So happy to send over some examples of some things that we worked on there.
Okay, cool.
Sounds good.
Yeah.
Any other...
That was all the big pieces that I wanted to flag.
As far as, like, any other questions, concerns, things you guys are excited to see so far, things you guys want to see better.
I know we've talked about, like, working on improving AEO and LLMs, continuing to grow there.
You know, the more, you the more effort we...
Or the more progress we can have in terms of continuing to drive traffic and seeing that as a strong engine.
Anything else top of mind for you guys?

Maybe thinking about how we can optimize our laning pages a little bit more to, based on your comment that you said in Slack, tracking leads that are coming through, maybe we just add a form to these blogs, so it's another place for convert.
I know we have our only embedded, but sometimes people don't always indicate that they're coming from blogs, obviously.
Yeah, I'm to know what you think about that.
Yeah, so we've done that a couple of different ways, and that's one of the optimizations that I want to look into.
You can add UTM tags to start tracking some specific events.
Sometimes, yeah, there's a specific form.
One of the things we're looking into in terms of tracking leads, depending, like, some groups.
We have Salesforce or HubSpot set up that tracks first or last touch attribution where they can see what content drove people to a demo page.
And there's a whole variety of different ways to set that up.
So it's one of the reasons I was asking where you guys track your leads and if you have information about the source.
It sounds like Calendly mostly, but do you guys have another tool that you're using right now?
No, not really.
but remind me, do we have the Calendly links embedded on the blog, like on the individual pages?
I know.
I don't think the Calendly, let look, we do put the link to the demo or link to Calendly sign up on the final CTA of blogs and it's in the podcast articles as well.
Guys, I have to jump to another call.
Yeah, no worries.
All right, I'll talk to you soon.
Yes.
Yes.
Yes.
So we've got it two places.
We've got it here on the Book of Strategy call.
So this CTA is on every post.
And then here on Let's Chat, this is also a link to the Calendly.
Okay.
Yeah, I don't know.
Like, I'm sure this would be kind of a hassle, but we can track UTMs on Calendly links.
So I wonder, is there any way we can just, like, whenever we deploy a new blog, just automatically append those UTMs to the Calendly link?
Just, like, which blog it is?
The source is just a blog?
Yeah, I think that's something really achievable that we can start to set up.
Okay.
Cool.
Yeah, I think that would be great.
And then, yeah, obviously I don't want to fill the blog with places to convert, but it is kind of at the bottom.
Like, they have to scroll all the way past the related articles.
I don't know if there's some way we can, like, just optimize, like...
And I guess the CRO of the blogs in a way.
Yeah, that's what I would just start to think about a little bit more too.
Yeah, totally.
Think about like adding some sort of sidebar, maybe under the categories that has a slightly tailored CTA of sorts.
I've seen it lots of different ways as folks start to optimize how their site's set up.
So I can do some brainstorming on that as well.
Yeah, that'd be awesome.
You you can use messaging like you'll meet directly with the founders or something like that, have our profiles, like stuff like that would be great.
Yeah, totally.
And if you've got any other ideas in that realm of things that you have seen on other sources, like I said, we'll do some digging and offer some recs.
But any ideas you have, open to it.
Yeah, yeah, for sure.
Cool.
That's everything I had.
Anything else top of mind for you?
No, that's it.
Cool.
Well, thanks for the time, Ali.
Because I know we've rescheduled a couple times.
Is this time better for you guys on Thursdays?
I'm happy to make this work.
It just depends.
I forget.
There was some conflict.
Oh, yeah.
I had a webinar during our normal times and so I couldn't make it today.
Let me check, you know, on the 5th.
It looks like both Alex and I are open on the 5th.
Okay.
So it should be good.
I don't think we don't have like a reoccurring call during this time.
It's just the webinar that I have to join.
Yeah.
All good.
I just want to make sure since we had it rescheduled a couple of different times.
Yeah, yeah.
Sweet.
I'll let you know if anything changes though.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Well, enjoy the rest of your week.
I will send over, I've got a couple of key links that I want to make sure that you guys have visibility in the chat again.
And otherwise, yeah.
Enjoy your almost Friday.
Yeah.
I appreciate it.
You too.
Yeah.
No worries.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review content performance and align on Q1 strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=125.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Performance is strong:** Post-holiday sessions hit an all-time high, and keyword rankings (positions 2–10) are skyrocketing, validating the content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
  - [**LLM traffic is highly engaged:** Users referred from LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT) have higher dwell times than those from Google, confirming that AI is effectively matching content to relevant audiences.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=308.0)
  - [**Reddit strategy is approved:** A new content pipeline will launch in mid-to-late February to build karma and establish a presence on the platform.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1237.0)
  - [**Lead tracking is the next priority:** UTMs will be added to Calendly links in blog CTAs to attribute leads directly to specific articles.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1753.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance & Optimization

  - [**Performance Highlights (3-Month Review):**](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
      - [**Sessions:** Post-holiday traffic hit an all-time high, fully recovering from a minor seasonal dip.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=243.0)
      - [**Keyword Rankings:** First-page rankings (positions 2–10) have skyrocketed, showing Google is recognizing site authority.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
      - [**LLM Referrals:** Traffic from LLMs is growing and shows higher engagement (dwell time) than Google traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=308.0)
  - [**High-Performing Content:**](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)
      - [**"Trends & Benchmark" Content:** Extremely popular (e.g., "benchmark conversion rate").](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)
      - [**"B2B SaaS Growth Strategy" Content:** Also performing very well.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)
  - [**Future Optimization:**](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1270.0)
      - [**Content Refresh:** In March, refresh existing articles with TLDRs and FAQs to improve LLM visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1270.0)
      - [**New Content:** Focus keyword research on the high-performing "benchmark" and "growth strategy" topics.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=452.0)

### Reddit Strategy

  - [**Goal:** Establish a presence on Reddit to drive traffic and build authority.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
  - [**Timeline:** Content pipeline launches mid-to-late February.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1237.0)
  - [**Execution:**](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=948.0)
      - [**Account Setup:** Understory to accept GrowthX's email transfer request and provide a banner image (size to be provided by GrowthX).](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=948.0)
      - [**Content Cadence:** GrowthX will post weekly articles to the Understory profile.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1186.0)
      - [**Strategy:** Build karma before posting in subreddits to avoid bot flags and bans.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1086.0)
      - [**Reporting:** GrowthX is developing a Reddit-specific reporting method.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1226.0)

### New Initiatives & Lead Tracking

  - [**Lead Tracking:**](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1753.0)
      - [**Problem:** Inability to attribute leads from blog CTAs to specific articles.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1753.0)
      - [**Solution:** Add UTMs to Calendly links in all blog CTAs.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1877.0)
      - [**Future Idea:** Test a more prominent, tailored CTA (e.g., sidebar) to improve conversion rates.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1906.0)
  - [**Backlinks:**](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1535.0)
      - [**GrowthX Service:** GrowthX is investigating a service to secure \~5 backlinks/month via a partner network. Pricing is pending.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1535.0)
      - [**Understory Initiative:** Understory will leverage its own network for informal backlink asks. GrowthX will provide messaging templates.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1610.0)
  - [**New Service Keyword Research:**](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1432.0)
      - [**LinkedIn Thought Leadership:** GrowthX will research keywords for this upcoming service once Understory provides messaging.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1432.0)
      - [**HubSpot & Outbound Sync:** GrowthX will add keywords for these tools to the content calendar.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1477.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=948.0)
      - [Resend Reddit account ownership transfer request.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=961.0)
      - [Provide Reddit banner image size specs.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1015.0)
      - [Deliver content audit with new keyword list by end of next week.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1369.0)
      - [Confirm pricing and details for backlink service by next week.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1535.0)
      - [Provide backlink request messaging templates for Understory's use.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1661.0)
      - [Brainstorm and propose blog CRO optimizations (e.g., sidebar CTA).](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1906.0)
      - [Investigate the 82 unindexed pages in Google Search Console.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=853.0)
  - [**Understory:**](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=938.0)
      - [Accept Reddit account ownership transfer.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=961.0)
      - [Provide banner image for Reddit profile.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1015.0)
      - [Review new keyword list and flag any topics to remove.](https://fathom.video/share/9kzNocwKrgx6eKXScxjTNYfnz9DQzTgz?tab=summary&timestamp=1416.0)
